Project log

Project log

Metabolomics Pilot Experiment with Rafa (Arafath Kaja Najumudeen)

Metabolomics Pilot Experiment with Rafa (Arafath Kaja Najumudeen)

In total, 66 samples were sent to Rafa's lab for metabolomics analysis. The samples were selected with two different parameters:

In total, 66 samples were sent to Rafa's lab for metabolomics analysis. The samples were selected with two different parameters:

Ada's cohort: Comparing long and short (already relapsed) PFS samples.

Ada's cohort: Comparing long and short (already relapsed) PFS samples.

HRD (BRCAmut) samples matched with HRP (BRCAwt) samples. These samples were matched based on anatomical location (e.g., Adn/Ova or Ome samples) and tumor purity (max 20% difference).

HRD (BRCAmut) samples matched with HRP (BRCAwt) samples. These samples were matched based on anatomical location (e.g., Adn/Ova or Ome samples) and tumor purity (max 20% difference).

All HRD patients with Ome or Ova/Adn samples received by Oncosys-OVA were included. If the same patient had multiple Ome or Ova/Adn samples, the one with the highest tumor purity was selected.

All HRD patients with Ome or Ova/Adn samples received by Oncosys-OVA were included. If the same patient had multiple Ome or Ova/Adn samples, the one with the highest tumor purity was selected.

A total of 23 HRD samples were included, with 14 Ome samples and 9 Ova/Adn samples. These HRD samples were then matched with HRP samples from the same anatomical location (either Ome or Ova/Adn) and similar tumor purity (max 20% difference)..

A total of 23 HRD samples were included, with 14 Ome samples and 9 Ova/Adn samples. These HRD samples were then matched with HRP samples from the same anatomical location (either Ome or Ova/Adn) and similar tumor purity (max 20% difference)..

See samples: Pilot exp samples March 2026

See samples: Pilot exp samples March 2026

Instructions by Rafa for preparing the samples:

Instructions by Rafa for preparing the samples:

Take a 10-20mg piece of snap-frozen tissue and place it in a cold Eppendorf tube on dry ice.

Take a 10-20mg piece of snap-frozen tissue and place it in a cold Eppendorf tube on dry ice.

We can weigh the samples here before analysis if you only take a piece of tissue in the Eppendorf tube.

We can weigh the samples here before analysis if you only take a piece of tissue in the Eppendorf tube.

Keep the temperature as close to -80°C as possible to prevent the samples from thawing.

Keep the temperature as close to -80°C as possible to prevent the samples from thawing.

Use consistent labeling for tracking purposes.

Use consistent labeling for tracking purposes.

Store the samples at -80°C, and we will take over from there.

Store the samples at -80°C, and we will take over from there.

Sample preparations:

Sample preparations:

Tubes were pre-labeled as follows:

Tubes were pre-labeled as follows:

Lid: Sample ID

Lid: Sample ID

Side: Sample ID, 16.03.2026 (cutting date), AE (initials)

Side: Sample ID, 16.03.2026 (cutting date), AE (initials)

Pre-labeled tubes and snap-frozen material were transferred to dry ice.

Pre-labeled tubes and snap-frozen material were transferred to dry ice.

Cutting was done in batches of ~15 samples.

Cutting was done in batches of ~15 samples.

A 10-20mg piece was cut from the snap-frozen tissue using a scalpel on a 10cm Petri dish.

A 10-20mg piece was cut from the snap-frozen tissue using a scalpel on a 10cm Petri dish.

The leftover snap-frozen tissue was returned to the original tube, and a new small piece was placed in a new, labeled tube. All tubes were immediately transferred to dry ice.

The leftover snap-frozen tissue was returned to the original tube, and a new small piece was placed in a new, labeled tube. All tubes were immediately transferred to dry ice.

Between samples, the scalpel was cleaned with 70% ethanol. A new section of the Petri dish was used for each sample, or a new dish was used if there wasn’t enough space on the existing one. This was to minimize cross-contamination between samples.

Between samples, the scalpel was cleaned with 70% ethanol. A new section of the Petri dish was used for each sample, or a new dish was used if there wasn’t enough space on the existing one. This was to minimize cross-contamination between samples.

Once cutting was complete, all tubes were returned to the -80°C freezer.

Once cutting was complete, all tubes were returned to the -80°C freezer.
